// - Matheus Cavalcante <matheusd@iis.ee.ethz.ch>

// NOTE: The upsizer does not support WRAP bursts, and will answer with SLVERR
// upon receiving a burst of such type. In addition to that, the downsizer also
// does not support FIXED bursts with incoming axlen != 0.

module axi_dw_converter #(
    parameter int unsigned AxiMaxReads         = 1    , // Number of outstanding reads
    parameter int unsigned AxiSlvPortDataWidth = 8    , // Data width of the slv port
    parameter int unsigned AxiMstPortDataWidth = 8    , // Data width of the mst port
    parameter int unsigned AxiAddrWidth        = 1    , // Address width
    parameter int unsigned AxiIdWidth          = 1    , // ID width
    parameter type aw_chan_t                   = logic, // AW Channel Type
    parameter type mst_w_chan_t                = logic, //  W Channel Type for the mst port
    parameter type slv_w_chan_t                = logic, //  W Channel Type for the slv port
    parameter type b_chan_t                    = logic, //  B Channel Type
    parameter type ar_chan_t                   = logic, // AR Channel Type
    parameter type mst_r_chan_t                = logic, //  R Channel Type for the mst port
    parameter type slv_r_chan_t                = logic, //  R Channel Type for the slv port
    parameter type axi_mst_req_t               = logic, // AXI Request Type for mst ports
    parameter type axi_mst_resp_t              = logic, // AXI Response Type for mst ports
    parameter type axi_slv_req_t               = logic, // AXI Request Type for slv ports
    parameter type axi_slv_resp_t              = logic  // AXI Response Type for slv ports
  ) (
    input  logic          clk_i,
    input  logic          rst_ni,
    // Slave interface
    input  axi_slv_req_t  slv_req_i,
    output axi_slv_resp_t slv_resp_o,
    // Master interface
    output axi_mst_req_t  mst_req_o,
    input  axi_mst_resp_t mst_resp_i
  );

  if (AxiMstPortDataWidth == AxiSlvPortDataWidth) begin: gen_no_dw_conversion
    assign mst_req_o  = slv_req_i ;
    assign slv_resp_o = mst_resp_i;
  end : gen_no_dw_conversion

  if (AxiMstPortDataWidth > AxiSlvPortDataWidth) begin: gen_dw_upsize
    axi_dw_upsizer #(
      .AxiMaxReads        (AxiMaxReads        ),
      .AxiSlvPortDataWidth(AxiSlvPortDataWidth),
      .AxiMstPortDataWidth(AxiMstPortDataWidth),
      .AxiAddrWidth       (AxiAddrWidth       ),
      .AxiIdWidth         (AxiIdWidth         ),
      .aw_chan_t          (aw_chan_t          ),
      .mst_w_chan_t       (mst_w_chan_t       ),
      .slv_w_chan_t       (slv_w_chan_t       ),
      .b_chan_t           (b_chan_t           ),
      .ar_chan_t          (ar_chan_t          ),
      .mst_r_chan_t       (mst_r_chan_t       ),
      .slv_r_chan_t       (slv_r_chan_t       ),
      .axi_mst_req_t      (axi_mst_req_t      ),
      .axi_mst_resp_t     (axi_mst_resp_t     ),
      .axi_slv_req_t      (axi_slv_req_t      ),
      .axi_slv_resp_t     (axi_slv_resp_t     )
    ) i_axi_dw_upsizer (
      .clk_i     (clk_i     ),
      .rst_ni    (rst_ni    ),
      // Slave interface
      .slv_req_i (slv_req_i ),
      .slv_resp_o(slv_resp_o),
      // Master interface
      .mst_req_o (mst_req_o ),
      .mst_resp_i(mst_resp_i)
    );
  end : gen_dw_upsize

  if (AxiMstPortDataWidth < AxiSlvPortDataWidth) begin: gen_dw_downsize
    axi_dw_downsizer #(
      .AxiMaxReads        (AxiMaxReads        ),
      .AxiSlvPortDataWidth(AxiSlvPortDataWidth),
      .AxiMstPortDataWidth(AxiMstPortDataWidth),
      .AxiAddrWidth       (AxiAddrWidth       ),
      .AxiIdWidth         (AxiIdWidth         ),
      .aw_chan_t          (aw_chan_t          ),
      .mst_w_chan_t       (mst_w_chan_t       ),
      .slv_w_chan_t       (slv_w_chan_t       ),
      .b_chan_t           (b_chan_t           ),
      .ar_chan_t          (ar_chan_t          ),
      .mst_r_chan_t       (mst_r_chan_t       ),
      .slv_r_chan_t       (slv_r_chan_t       ),
      .axi_mst_req_t      (axi_mst_req_t      ),
      .axi_mst_resp_t     (axi_mst_resp_t     ),
      .axi_slv_req_t      (axi_slv_req_t      ),
      .axi_slv_resp_t     (axi_slv_resp_t     )
    ) i_axi_dw_downsizer (
      .clk_i     (clk_i     ),
      .rst_ni    (rst_ni    ),
      // Slave interface
      .slv_req_i (slv_req_i ),
      .slv_resp_o(slv_resp_o),
      // Master interface
      .mst_req_o (mst_req_o ),
      .mst_resp_i(mst_resp_i)
    );
  end : gen_dw_downsize

endmodule : axi_dw_converter

// Interface wrapper

`include "axi/assign.svh"
`include "axi/typedef.svh"

module axi_dw_converter_intf #(
    parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH            = 1,
    parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH          = 1,
    parameter int unsigned AXI_SLV_PORT_DATA_WIDTH = 8,
    parameter int unsigned AXI_MST_PORT_DATA_WIDTH = 8,
    parameter int unsigned AXI_USER_WIDTH          = 0,
    parameter int unsigned AXI_MAX_READS           = 8
  ) (
    input          logic clk_i,
    input          logic rst_ni,
    AXI_BUS.Slave        slv,
    AXI_BUS.Master       mst
  );

  typedef logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0] id_t                   ;
  typedef logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] addr_t               ;
  typedef logic [AXI_MST_PORT_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] mst_data_t  ;
  typedef logic [AXI_MST_PORT_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0] mst_strb_t;
  typedef logic [AXI_SLV_PORT_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] slv_data_t  ;
  typedef logic [AXI_SLV_PORT_DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0] slv_strb_t;
  typedef logic [AXI_USER_WIDTH-1:0] user_t               ;
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(aw_chan_t, addr_t, id_t, user_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(mst_w_chan_t, mst_data_t, mst_strb_t, user_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(slv_w_chan_t, slv_data_t, slv_strb_t, user_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(b_chan_t, id_t, user_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(ar_chan_t, addr_t, id_t, user_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(mst_r_chan_t, mst_data_t, id_t, user_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(slv_r_chan_t, slv_data_t, id_t, user_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(mst_req_t, aw_chan_t, mst_w_chan_t, ar_chan_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(mst_resp_t, b_chan_t, mst_r_chan_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(slv_req_t, aw_chan_t, slv_w_chan_t, ar_chan_t)
  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(slv_resp_t, b_chan_t, slv_r_chan_t)

  slv_req_t  slv_req;
  slv_resp_t slv_resp;
  mst_req_t  mst_req;
  mst_resp_t mst_resp;

  `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(slv_req, slv)
  `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv, slv_resp)

  `AXI_ASSIGN_FROM_REQ(mst, mst_req)
  `AXI_ASSIGN_TO_RESP(mst_resp, mst)

  axi_dw_converter #(
    .AxiMaxReads        ( AXI_MAX_READS           ),
    .AxiSlvPortDataWidth( AXI_SLV_PORT_DATA_WIDTH ),
    .AxiMstPortDataWidth( AXI_MST_PORT_DATA_WIDTH ),
    .AxiAddrWidth       ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH          ),
    .AxiIdWidth         ( AXI_ID_WIDTH            ),
    .aw_chan_t          ( aw_chan_t               ),
    .mst_w_chan_t       ( mst_w_chan_t            ),
    .slv_w_chan_t       ( slv_w_chan_t            ),
    .b_chan_t           ( b_chan_t                ),
    .ar_chan_t          ( ar_chan_t               ),
    .mst_r_chan_t       ( mst_r_chan_t            ),
    .slv_r_chan_t       ( slv_r_chan_t            ),
    .axi_mst_req_t      ( mst_req_t               ),
    .axi_mst_resp_t     ( mst_resp_t              ),
    .axi_slv_req_t      ( slv_req_t               ),
    .axi_slv_resp_t     ( slv_resp_t              )
  ) i_axi_dw_converter (
    .clk_i      ( clk_i    ),
    .rst_ni     ( rst_ni   ),
    // slave port
    .slv_req_i  ( slv_req  ),
    .slv_resp_o ( slv_resp ),
    // master port
    .mst_req_o  ( mst_req  ),
    .mst_resp_i ( mst_resp )
  );

endmodule : axi_dw_converter_intf

286	  ///   successful.
287	  /// - Both DECERR and SLVERR mean (part of) a transaction were unsuccessful, whereas OKAY means an

content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the module, the testbench, and how it's run. Let me now write the tutorial lecture.

Let me organize my understanding of the key concepts for this lecture:

## `axi_dw_downsizer`: 宽到窄数据宽度转换

### Overall structure
- `axi_dw_downsizer` is the core (struct-based, Level 4)
- `axi_dw_converter` (Level 4) is the dispatcher: it picks upsize/downsize/passthrough based on data width comparison
- `axi_dw_converter_intf` is the interface wrapper

### Key parameters
- `AxiMaxReads` - number of outstanding reads (= number of parallel read downsizers)
- `AxiSlvPortDataWidth` - wide (slave/upstream) data width
- `AxiMstPortDataWidth` - narrow (master/downstream) data width
- Note: in this library "slave port" = upstream/wide side, "master port" = downstream/narrow side. The downsizer sits between a wide master (the device that talks to the downsizer's slave port) and a narrow slave (the device the downsizer's master port drives).

Wait, let me reconsider the terminology. The downsizer's slave port faces upstream (the wide bus master), and its master port faces downstream (the narrow slave). So:
- slv port data width = wide
- mst port data width = narrow

The module "Connects a wide master to a narrower slave" — i.e. the wide master connects to the downsizer's slave port, and the downsizer's master port connects to the narrow slave.

### Key derived constants
- `AxiSlvPortStrbWidth = AxiSlvPortDataWidth / 8`
- `AxiMstPortStrbWidth = AxiMstPortDataWidth / 8`
- `AxiSlvPortMaxSize = $clog2(AxiSlvPortStrbWidth)` - max size on wide side
- `AxiMstPortMaxSize = $clog2(AxiMstPortStrbWidth)` - max size on narrow side
- `SlvPortByteMask`, `MstPortByteMask`
- Byte-grouped types: `mst_data_t = logic [AxiMstPortStrbWidth-1:0][7:0]`, `slv_data_t`

### Conversion ratio
`conv_ratio = ((1 << size) + AxiMstPortStrbWidth - 1) / AxiMstPortStrbWidth`
This is ceil(num_bytes(size) / AxiMstPortStrbWidth) — how many narrow beats are needed to carry one wide beat of the given size. If conv_ratio == 1, no downsizing needed (passthrough).

### Architecture
The module has three big pieces:
1. **Arbiters** (R, AR) and an internal demux + error slave
2. **Read path** (`gen_read_downsizer`): AxiMaxReads parallel instances, each an FSM (R_IDLE, R_INJECT_AW, R_PASSTHROUGH, R_INCR_DOWNSIZE, R_SPLIT_INCR_DOWNSIZE)
3. **Write path**: single FSM (W_IDLE, W_PASSTHROUGH, W_INCR_DOWNSIZE, W_SPLIT_INCR_DOWNSIZE)

Let me detail each.

### Arbiter structure
- `i_slv_r_arb`: rr_arb_tree that merges AxiMaxReads R-channels from the read downsizers back into one R output to the slave (upstream) side.
- `i_slv_ar_arb`: 2-input arbiter between normal AR and "inject AW into AR" (for ATOP with R response). Picks which ID goes to the read downsizers.
- `i_mst_ar_arb`: rr_arb_tree that merges AxiMaxReads AR-channels from the read downsizers into one AR output to the master (downstream) side.

### Demux + error slave
- `i_axi_demux` with NoMstPorts=2 routes downstream requests either to the real narrow master port or to `i_axi_err_slv` (returns SLVERR). `mst_req_ar_err` and `mst_req_aw_err` select routing when a transaction can't be downsized (WRAP, multi-beat FIXED).
- SpillAw=1 is required to break dependency between AW and W channels.

### Read path details
Each of the AxiMaxReads read downsizers:
- Has a state machine tracking the read.
- `idle_read_downsizer[t]` is true when in R_IDLE or R_INJECT_AW.
- A `lzc` finds an idle downsizer for a new AR (`idx_idle_downsizer`).
- `id_clash_downsizer` detects if another downsizer is already handling the same ID — to maintain ordering, the new AR must go to the same downsizer. `idx_ar_downsizer` picks clash if any, else idle.
- `id_queue` (`i_read_id_queue`) maps each outstanding read's ID to which downsizer handles it, so R responses (keyed by ID) get routed back to the correct downsizer.

The R FSM states:
- R_IDLE: waiting for new AR.
- R_INJECT_AW: an ATOP (atomic with R response) was injected; prepare an internal "AR" from the AW fields.
- R_PASSTHROUGH: conv_ratio==1, forward beats directly.
- R_INCR_DOWNSIZE: one narrow burst per wide beat region.
- R_SPLIT_INCR_DOWNSIZE: the downsize burst would exceed 256 beats (AXI max len=255), so split into multiple INCR bursts.

The burst length calculation (the key formula):
```
size_mask = (1 << size) - 1
conv_ratio = ceil(num_bytes(size) / AxiMstPortStrbWidth)
align_adj = (addr & size_mask & ~MstPortByteMask) / AxiMstPortStrbWidth
burst_len = (len + 1) * conv_ratio - align_adj - 1
```
- `(len+1)` = number of beats in original wide burst.
- `*conv_ratio` = each wide beat becomes conv_ratio narrow beats.
- `-align_adj` = the first wide beat may be unaligned to the narrow width, so fewer narrow beats carry valid data at the start.
- `-1` = AXI len = beats-1.

If burst_len <= 255, single INCR downsize. Else split: first burst len = 255 - align_adj, rest in 255 chunks.

### Lane steering (write) / Serialization (read)
On the write path, "lane steering": each wide W beat is split across conv_ratio narrow W beats. For each byte lane b in the wide word:
```
if ((b >= slv_port_offset) &&
    (b - slv_port_offset < (1 << orig_aw_size)) &&
    (b + mst_port_offset - slv_port_offset < AxiMstPortStrbWidth))
  w_data[b + mst_port_offset - slv_port_offset] = slv_req_i.w.data[8*b +: 8];
  mst_req.w.strb[...] = slv_req_i.w.strb[b];
```
- `slv_port_offset` = byte offset within the wide word (low bits of addr).
- `mst_port_offset` = byte offset within the narrow word.
- Only bytes within the transfer size (`1 << orig_aw_size`) are steered.

On the read path, the inverse "serialization": narrow R beats are assembled into wide R beats.

### B channel handling
Each wide write may trigger multiple narrow B beats (one per narrow burst). The downsizer uses `i_forward_b_beats_queue` (fifo_v3) to count how many B beats a write will produce, and only forwards the **last** B beat to the upstream slave. The `burst_resp` accumulates errors via `resp_precedence`.

### R channel response merging
`r_req_d.r.resp = resp_precedence(r_req_q.r.resp, mst_resp.r.resp)` — merges multiple narrow R responses into one.

### WRAP / FIXED handling
- WRAP: not supported → throw_error → routed to err_slv → SLVERR.
- FIXED: only single-beat (len==0) supported; multi-beat FIXED → throw_error → SLVERR.

### Testbench
- `tb_axi_dw_downsizer` uses `axi_dw_converter_intf` as DUT (so it tests through the dispatcher).
- `axi_rand_master` on the wide side (slv port), `axi_rand_slave` on the narrow side (mst port).
- `tb_axi_dw_pkg::axi_dw_downsizer_monitor` snoops both ports and validates no beats are lost — it computes expected narrow transactions from the wide ones.
- `b_stall`/`r_stall` counters inject initial backpressure stress.
- run via `run_vsim.sh` config matrix: many width combinations + two stalling configs.

### Helper functions used
- `num_bytes(size)` = 1 << size
- `aligned_addr(addr, size)` = (addr >> size) << size
- `modifiable(cache)` = cache & CACHE_MODIFIABLE
- `resp_precedence(a, b)` = merge responses per DECERR > SLVERR > OKAY > EXOKAY

One thing about terminology: I need to clarify the slave/master port naming. In this library:
- The downsizer's **slave port** (slv) = upstream side = WIDE data (faces the wide bus master that drives the downsizer).
- The downsizer's **master port** (mst) = downstream side = NARROW data (drives the narrow slave).

So `AxiSlvPortDataWidth > AxiMstPortDataWidth` for the downsizer. This is consistent with the converter dispatcher check `AxiMstPortDataWidth < AxiSlvPortDataWidth` → downsize (L81).

Let me also double check the conv_ratio formula. In the source (L416):
`conv_ratio = ((1 << r_req_d.ar.size) + AxiMstPortStrbWidth - 1) / AxiMstPortStrbWidth;`
= ceil( (1<<size) / AxiMstPortStrbWidth ) = ceil( num_bytes(size) / AxiMstPortStrbWidth )

num_bytes(size) = 1<<size = bytes per beat on the wide side for this size. AxiMstPortStrbWidth = narrow port bytes. So conv_ratio = how many narrow beats to carry one wide-sized beat. Good. If size <= AxiMstPortMaxSize, then 1<<size <= AxiMstPortStrbWidth, conv_ratio=1, passthrough.

align_adj (L419):
`align_adj = (r_req_d.ar.addr & size_mask & ~MstPortByteMask) / AxiMstPortStrbWidth;`
- `size_mask = (1<<size)-1` masks the bytes within the size.
- `~MstPortByteMask` = ~(AxiMstPortStrbWidth-1) = high bits beyond the narrow port width.
- `addr & size_mask` = byte offset of the start within its size-aligned group.
- `& ~MstPortByteMask` keeps only the bits above the narrow-port boundary (i.e., how far into the wide beat we are, measured in narrow-word chunks beyond the first).
- `/ AxiMstPortStrbWidth` converts to narrow-beat count.
This is the number of "leading" narrow beats that don't carry data because the start address is unaligned to the narrow width. Equivalent to the monitor's `aligned_adjustment`.

burst_len (L420):
`r_req_d.burst_len = (r_req_d.ar.len + 1) * conv_ratio - align_adj - 1;`
- (len+1) wide beats × conv_ratio narrow beats each = total narrow beats if fully aligned.
- minus align_adj leading partial beats.
- minus 1 because len encoding is beats-1.
